1.title: Preparing for School
paras: Every morning, Cindy prepares for school. She gathers up all her homework and any books that she needs for the day. She then packs it all into her backpack. She also packs her pencil case, a snack and a jacket, in case she gets cold during the day. By the time she is finished packing, the backpack is quite heavy, but she has everything she needs for the day.

When packed and ready to go, she slings the backpack over her shoulder and heads for the door. She must get tired of carrying that heavy bag around all the time. At least she doesn’t have to worry about getting enough exercise.
questions: What do you carry in your school bag every day?
What type of school bag do you prefer, a backpack or a regular book bag?
Do you get tired of carrying your school bag around?
1.translation: 每天早上辛蒂準備上學，她收好所有的作業和當天需要的書，然後把它們裝進背包。她也把鉛筆盒、點心，以及一件外套放進去，以免白天的時候會冷。等她裝好之後，背包已經很重了，不過她已經有了當天所需要的每樣東西。 當她裝好書包準備上學時，她把背包掛在肩膀上，然後朝門的方向走去。對於老是得背那麼重的背包到處走，她一定感到很厭煩，不過，至少她不用擔心運動量不夠的問題。
answers: I usually carry a pencil case and any books I need for the day in my school bag.
I prefer a backpack because it is safer to carry when I ride my bike.
Yes, I’m tired of carrying my school bag around.

1.title: Jason Wipes His Nose
paras: Jason has a runny nose. He reaches for a box of tissue on the table so that he can wipe it. His mother told him that he should use tissue and not the sleeve of his shirt because that is very disgusting. A single tissue is all he needs and he plucks one out of the box.

First he wipes his nose, but it still feels plugged so he covers his nose with the tissue and blows. It sounds like he is playing the trumpet. After doing this a couple of times, his nose is cleared and he can breathe much better.

Jason is a slob. 16  He leaves the germ-laden, 18 dirty tissue on the table.  This is not the most hygienic of practices as someone else could catch his cold by touching it, but Jason is lazy and doesn’t care. His mother is always picking up after him. Will he ever learn?
questions: Should you wipe your nose on your sleeve?
How does Jason clear his nose so that he can breathe better?
What should you do with a dirty tissue?
1.translation: 傑森流鼻涕了，他伸手去拿桌子上的面紙來擦鼻涕。他的媽媽以前就告訴他應該要用面紙擦鼻涕，而不是用襯衫的袖子，因為那樣是非常噁心的。他只需要一張面紙就夠了，他從盒子中抽出一張。 一開始，他先擦他的鼻子，但仍然感覺鼻塞，所以他用面紙掩住鼻子，並用力的吹氣，聲音聽起來很像在吹小喇叭。當他擤個兩三次之後，他的鼻子通了，而且呼吸也順暢多了。 傑森是個不愛乾淨的人，他把充滿細菌的面紙留在桌子上。這可不是個最衛生的習慣，因為其他的人若接觸到的話，可能會染上他的感冒。由於他太懶了，所以他並不在乎。他的媽媽總是幫他收拾善後。他以後會學乖嗎？
answers: No, because it’s disgusting.
He blows his nose with a tissue.
You should throw it in the garbage can.

1.title: Wendell’s Big Bubble Trouble
paras: Wendell’s grandmother always brings a treat for him when she comes to visit. This time she has brought him a pack of gum. Wendell thanks his grandma and then unwraps a piece of the bubblegum and stuffs it into his mouth. It is grape flavor, which is his favorite.

As he chews his big wad of gum, his little sister, Lily, asks if she can have some, too. Wendell is a very selfish boy and doesn’t like to share. He continues to chew his gum and blow bubbles as Lily looks on with envy. She is very sad. Eventually, she gives up and goes crying to her mother.

Meanwhile, Wendell keeps blowing bubbles as if nothing had ever happened. He couldn’t care less about anyone’s feelings other than his own. Then just as he is blowing the biggest bubble of his life, it pops in his face. The sticky gum flies everywhere. It’s on the floor, on his clothes, on his face and even in his hair. Perhaps after treating his sister so poorly, karma has snuck up behind him and burst his bubble.
questions: What treat did Wendell’s grandmother bring for him?
Does Wendell share his treat with Lily?
Where does the gum go when the big bubble pops?
1.translation: 溫德爾的祖母來訪時，總會帶點零嘴點心給他。這回她帶給溫德爾的是一條口香糖。他向祖母謝謝後，隨即打開一塊泡泡糖，並且將它塞進嘴裡。這塊泡泡糖是葡萄口味的，這是他最愛的口味。 當他咀嚼著大塊的口香糖時，他的小妹莉莉問他可不可以給她一些。溫德爾是個非常自私的男孩，他不喜歡和別人分享。儘管莉莉在旁邊羨慕的看著，他仍繼續咀嚼他的口香糖，並且還吹著泡泡。她非常難過，最後她只好放棄，跑去向媽媽哭訴。 這時溫德爾若無其事地繼續吹著泡泡。他只在乎自己，完全不理會別人的感受。正當他吹著有生以來最大的泡泡時，泡泡砰的一聲在他臉上破掉了。黏黏的口香糖飛得到處都是，沾在地板、衣服、臉上，甚至沾在他的頭髮上。也許這是因為他對妹妹很不好，所以招致惡果偷偷溜到他的背後將泡泡戳破。(註：此處「惡果」採擬人化用法。)
answers: She brought a pack of gum for him.
No, because he is a very selfish boy.
It’s on the floor, on his clothes, on his face and even in his hair.

1.title: Who’s at the Door?
paras: Buzz! The doorbell is ringing. Myles wonders who it is as he races for the intercom.
“Who is it?” he inquires.
“Pizza delivery,” a strange voice replies.
“I didn’t order any pizza,” Myles claims.  “Are you sure you have the right address?”
“Is this number 63 Julin Street?” he asks.
“Uh, yeah.”
“Then I have ten pepperoni pizzas for you,” the voice insists.
“There must be a mistake! I didn’t order any pizza!” Myles argues.
The voice on the other end breaks into a roar of laughter.
“Gotcha!” Carl shouts.
“Carl, you idiot!” Myles says, breathing a sigh of relief.  “You really had me going.”
“Yeah, I know,” Carl snorts. “Buzz me up so we can watch TV.”
Myles presses a button on the intercom and the front door unlocks. He then runs to hide so that he can pay back Carl by scaring him.
questions: Does Myles know who is at the door?
Who does his friend claim to be?
Why does Myles run and hide after he unlocks the door?
1.translation: 鈴...！門鈴響起。麥爾斯跑去接對講機時，心裡在想到底會是誰呢？ 「請問是哪位？」他問。 「送披薩的，」一個陌生的聲音回答他。 麥爾斯說：「我沒有訂任何的披薩，你確定住址正確嗎？」 「這是竹林街63號嗎？」他問。 「嗯，是的！」 「那我有十個義大利辣味香腸披薩要給你，」這個人堅持地說。 「一定是出了差錯，我沒有訂任何的披薩啊！」麥爾斯辯稱。 對講機的另一頭傳出了一陣爆笑聲。 「上當了！」卡爾大叫。 「卡爾你這個白癡，」麥爾斯鬆了一口氣說：「我真的被你騙了。」 「是啊，我知道，」卡爾嘲諷地大笑。「幫我開門吧，我們一起看電視。」 麥爾斯按了對講機的按鈕，前門就打開了。接著他跑去躲起來，以便要嚇嚇卡爾來報復他。
answers: No, he doesn’t know who is at the door.
His friend claims to be a pizza delivery boy.
He runs to hide because he wants to scare Carl.

1.title: Naptime at School
paras: The teacher in this classroom must be extremely boring, as all the children have fallen asleep. Actually, it is part of the daily routine for most Taiwanese school children. From 12:00 till 12:30 they eat their lunch. This is followed by a nap that lasts until about 1:20. Some teachers believe that by having the children take a short nap after lunch they will be refreshed and ready to learn for the second half of the day.  　　The standard position seems to be hunched over with your head resting on folded arms. Some students say that this is a very uncomfortable position and claim that it gives them sore arms. Others say that being hunched over while having a full belly makes them feel a bit sick. Regardless of 22 whether or not they like it, it is the policy of some teachers. I hope those students who don’t like it get used to it, as there is no room in the classroom or the school budget for dozens of 28 beds.
questions: Why are all the children sleeping?
What is the problem with sleeping hunched over?
Why can’t the school buy beds?
1.translation: 這間教室的老師上課一定極為無聊，因為所有的小朋友都睡著了。事實上，這是大多數台灣學校小朋友每天例行公事的一部份。十二點到十二點半他們吃午餐，之後的休息持續到大約一點二十分。一些老師相信，讓這些學生吃完午餐後稍微小睡一下，可以讓他們恢復精神，然後準備好學習下午的課程。 午睡的標準姿勢似乎是趴著，然後頭靠在交叉的手臂上面。有些學生說這是非常不舒服的姿勢，並宣稱這個姿勢會讓手臂痠痛。其他學生則說肚子飽飽的時候趴著睡，會讓他們覺得有一點不舒服。不管他們喜不喜歡，這是一些老師的規定。我希望那些不喜歡的學生能夠適應它，因為教室沒有空間，或者學校也沒有預算能夠買幾十張床放在教室。
answers: Because it’s a part of their daily routine.
It’s a very uncomfortable position.
Because there’s no budget. Besides, there is no room in the classroom.

1.title: The School Library
paras: Students borrow a lot of books from libraries. Whether you are researching a topic for your homework or just need a book to relax with for the weekend, you can just check out a book from your school’s library.  You don’t even have to pay for it.

These students are browsing the shelves for a book, but there are so many to choose from that finding a book can be difficult.  All the books are organized into categories or alphabetized by author or title.  Still there are times when you just can’t find that specific book you’re looking for. In these instances, try using the library’s database to speed up your search.  It could save you a lot of time by locating the book for you.

If you decide to take a book home for a while, you will need to check it out. Simply take the book to the librarian. They will scan the book and your library card into the computer. This will give them a record of who has the book and when it is due back. Make sure your book comes back on time. In public libraries you can be charged a fee for any overdue books. If it happens too often, they may revoke your library card.
questions: What are some of the reasons students borrow books?
Why is a database useful for finding a book?
How do you check a book out?
1.translation: 學生從圖書館借很多書，不管你是為了寫作業而研究一個主題，或者只是想利用週末看書來放鬆心情，你都可以到學校的圖書館借書，甚至不用花你任何錢呢！ 這些學生正在瀏覽書架找書，但是因為有太多的書可供選擇，所以要找到一本書可能很困難。所有的書都有分類編排，或依照作者的姓名或書名的字母順序排列。然而，有時候你還是會找不到某本你要的書。遇到這種情況，你可以試著使用圖書館的資料庫來加快查詢速度；它可以找出書的位置，為你節省很多時間。 如果你決定要把書帶回家看一陣子，你必須要辦理借閱手續。只要把書交給圖書館員，他們就會把這本書以及你借書證的資料掃描到電腦中。這樣一來，他們便會有這本書被誰借走以及何時到期的記錄。請務必準時歸還書籍，在公立圖書館，如果你逾期還書可能會被罰錢。如果這種情形經常發生的話，他們也許會取消你的借書證。
answers: They borrow books to do their homework or to research things for papers.
It is useful because it can speed up your search.
You check it out by taking it to the librarian.

1.title: Grocery Shopping
paras: On her way home from school, Jenny decided she needed to stop at the local grocery store. As she entered, she looked around and found a shopping basket. She placed the basket on a cart to make a mini-shopping cart. It was easier for her to push the cart rather than carry the basket. The aisles were organized neatly and Jenny pushed the cart down each one to find what she needed. Each item she wanted she took off the shelf and placed in the shopping cart. Jenny, being a bargain hunter, preferred the items that were on sale. Her thriftiness allows her to save a lot of money whenever she shops.

After filling the basket, she lined up at the checkout. When it was her turn, she emptied the contents of the basket onto the counter. The cashier scanned each item. A couple items wouldn’t scan correctly so the cashier had to key in the bar code manually. When the cashier had finished scanning everything, she hit the total button. Jenny handed her a five hundred dollar bill. The cashier checked the bill to make sure it wasn’t counterfeit and then slipped it into the cash register. She then counted out the change and handed it back to Jenny. Jenny thanked her, took her bag of groceries and headed for home.
questions: Where was Jenny going when she decided to stop and buy groceries?
Why does Jenny prefer items that are on sale?
What was the purpose for checking the bill?
1.translation: 在從學校返家的途中，珍妮決定必須去一趟當地的雜貨店。當她一進門時，四周看了一下，並找到了一個購物籃。她把它放在推車上，就變成了一台迷你購物車。對她來說，推購物車比提籃子還要輕鬆。走道的東西排放地非常整齊。珍妮推著購物車，一排一排地尋找她需要的東西。她把想買的每樣東西從架上拿下來，然後放入購物車中。由於珍妮是一個喜歡購買便宜貨的人，所以她偏好特價商品。由於她的節儉，讓她每次購物時都能節省很多的錢。 當籃子裝滿東西後，她便到收銀台排隊結帳。輪到她的時候，她把籃子的東西全部拿到櫃台上面。收銀員將每件商品一一掃描，但是其中有一兩樣無法正確地掃描，所以收銀員必須用手輸入條碼。當她把每樣東西都掃描過後，便按了合計的按鍵。珍妮拿了一張五百元的鈔票給她，收銀員便檢查這張鈔票，以確認是否為偽鈔，接著她就把它塞入收銀機。她算出要找的零錢，然後把它交給了珍妮。珍妮對她說聲謝謝，便提著一包雜貨回家。
answers: She was going home.
Because she is a bargain hunter.
The purpose was to make sure it wasn’t counterfeit.

1.title: Dad’s New Car
paras: My dad bought a brand-new car yesterday. It is the coolest car ever. It has everything you could want, like nice soft seats, air-conditioning, power windows, and even a DVD player in the back seat. Silver wouldn’t have been my first choice for a color, but then again I didn’t have to pay for it, so I guess it’s ok.

I like the new car so much that I am going to ask my parents if I can live in it. That would be so cool. It has everything I could possibly want—except for maybe a bathroom. I can see myself sitting in it during a hot summer day, watching a movie, sipping a drink and cooling off with the air-conditioning blasting away. Life couldn’t get much better than that.

My little sister really likes the new car, too, but not as much as me. Her favorite part is the sunroof. Dad says we aren’t allowed to stick out our heads while the car is moving or they’ll be chopped off, but we still like to do it when the car is parked.

I am actually looking forward to long trips now. I used to hate them, but then again our old car was a piece of junk.  Whenever we went on a trip, the car would overheat and we weren’t allowed to use the air-con because that only made it heat up faster. We shouldn’t have to worry about that anymore;23 plus, we can watch movies. It’s like a dream come true.
questions: What is in the back seat of the car?
What doesn’t the car have that the boy might like?
Why couldn’t they use the air-con in the old car?
1.translation: 昨天我爸買了一台全新的車子。它是有史以來最酷的車子。任何你可能想要的配備它都有了，像是精巧又柔軟的座椅、空調、電動車窗，甚至後座還有DVD播放器。就顏色而言，銀色並不是我的第一選擇，但是話說回來，買車的錢並不是我出的，所以我想這是可以接受的。 我非常喜歡這台新車，喜歡到我想問我的父母是否可以住在這台車子裡面，那將會是很棒的。任何我可能想要的配備都在裡面了，也許除了廁所之外吧！我可以想像夏天的時候，我坐在裡面看電影、喝飲料，還吹著涼爽的冷氣。人生中沒有比這樣更美好的了！ 我的小妹也很喜歡這台車，但是沒有像我這麼喜歡。她最喜歡的部份是天窗。 爸爸說：當車子開動的時候，不准我們把頭伸出車外，不然頭會被切斷。但是，當車子停好時，我們仍然喜歡把頭伸出去。 我真的很期待去長途旅行。以前我很討厭長途旅行，話說回來，這是因為我們的舊車就像垃圾一樣爛，坐起來很不舒服。每次我們去旅行，車子都會過熱，而且我們不能使用空調，因為這會使溫度上升的更快。我們應該再也不必擔心這個問題了。此外，我們還可以看電影。這就像是美夢成真啊！
answers: A DVD player is in the back seat of the car.
It doesn’t have a bathroom.
Because the old car would overheat.

1.title: My Dog Rex
paras: This is my dog Rex. He is small with short legs and floppy ears. His coat is light brown with a few black patches. I don’t know what breed he is, but he looks like a beagle crossed with a typical Taiwanese mutt.

He was just a puppy when Dad found him on the street and was in very bad condition. It looked like he hadn’t eaten for days or longer, and was covered with ticks. My father took pity on him and brought him home.

Mom immediately took Rex to the vet to have him checked out and to make sure that he was ok. The vet gave him a couple of shots, a flea bath and treated a couple of sores on his legs. It cost quite a bit to have him taken care of, but it didn’t matter because our family couldn’t stand to let an animal suffer like that.
Once at home, he was a bit nervous, but settled down shortly after. He could tell that he was in a safe place.
The next day, Dad went out and bought him a doghouse. It was small, white, and had a peaked roof 20 just like a real house. Rex seemed to like it too and went in to sniff 21 around right away.

Dad also bought a collar and a leash. Most of the family is gone during the daytime, so we needed something to tie him up in the yard with. It restricted him a bit, but I don’t think he minded. He was happy to have a good home.

My family has had Rex for almost seven years now and I can’t imagine life without him. He is so happy to see us whenever we get home and always greets us with a big sloppy kiss. He is also great at cheering us up when we are feeling down. He is the best dog ever, and I hope he lives a long, long time.
questions: What kind of condition was the dog in when it was found?
Why didn’t it matter that it cost so much to take the dog to the vet?
What does Rex do to greet its family?
1.translation: 這是我的狗－雷克斯。他是一隻腿很短而且耳朵下垂的小狗。他的毛是淺棕色的，並且有一些黑色的斑點。我不知道他的品種，但他看起來像是小獵犬和傳統台灣土狗的混種。   當我爸在街上發現他時，他還很小，而且狀況非常不好。那時他看起來似乎有好幾天沒吃東西了，或甚至更久一點，而且全身都是蝨子。我爸看他很可憐，於是將他帶回家。   媽媽立刻帶他去獸醫那兒檢查，以確認他的狀況無虞。獸醫給他打了幾針、幫他洗澡清除跳蚤，並且治療他腳上的幾處傷口。照顧他花了我們不少錢，但這不重要，因為我們家人無法忍受讓一隻動物受那種苦。   剛到我家時，他有點緊張，但很快心就安定下來了。他知道他待的是一個安全的地方。 隔天，老爸外出幫他買了個狗屋，這個小狗屋顏色是白的，屋頂尖尖的，看起來像是真的房子。雷克斯似乎也很喜歡他的窩，並且馬上走進去到處嗅嗅。   爸爸也買了一個項圈和鍊條。白天時，大部分的家人都不在，所以我們需要東西來把他拴在院子裡。這有點限制了他的活動範圍，但我認為他不會介意，他很高興能有一個好的住所。   雷克斯來我們家已經快七年了，我無法想像沒有他的生活。每當我們回家時，他都很高興看到我們，而且總是用溼溼的舌頭熱情地舔著我們以示歡迎。當我們心情沮喪時，他也很會逗我們開心。他是有史以來最棒的狗，我希望他能活很久很久。
answers: It looked like he hadn’t eaten for days or longer, and was covered with ticks.
Because they couldn’t stand to let an animal suffer like that.
He always greets them with a big sloppy kiss.

1.title: Belinda Has a Flat
paras: Belinda’s back tire seemed a little flat as she rode to school this morning, so she is checking the air pressure in it. As she squeezes it with her hands, it feels noticeably soft. If she were to ride on it any further, it may damage the tire or tube.
To fill the tire up, she uses a small air pump. First, she attaches the air hose to the valve stem located between the spokes and locks it in place.

Next, she begins to fill the tube with air by pumping up and down on the handle. It takes only a few minutes before the tire is hard again, but there is a problem. Belinda hears a hissing sound coming from somewhere on the tire. Examining the tire more closely reveals a small nail that has punctured it. Before she can ride again, she will need to buy a new tube or patch the old one. For now, she will have to be content with walking.
questions: How does Belinda check the tire’s air pressure?
What would happen if Belinda rode the bike with a flat tire?
How does Belinda know that there is something wrong with the tire?
1.translation: 今天早上，布蘭達騎腳踏車上學時，她的後輪似乎有點洩氣了，所以她現在正在檢查胎壓。當她用手擠壓後輪時，輪胎感覺明顯變軟了。如果她再騎下去，腳踏車的外胎或內胎可能會受損。 她使用一個小型的打氣筒幫輪胎打氣。首先，她將送氣的軟管接到輪輻間的閥桿，並且把它固定好。 接著，她開始用把手上下施力為輪胎打氣。只花了幾分鐘的時間，輪胎就變硬了。但是有一個問題，那就是布蘭達聽到輪胎的某處發出嘶嘶聲。在更加仔細地檢查輪胎之後，發現有一根小鐵釘刺穿了輪胎。她將必須換新的內胎，或是把舊胎修補一下，才可以繼續騎。現在，她就只能安步當車了。
answers: She squeezes it with her hands.
It may damage the tire or tube.
She hears a hissing sound coming from somewhere on the tire.

2.title: Running Laps
paras: Our junior high school really believes in promoting physical exercise for the benefit of the students. Every morning after assembly, students are required to run three laps around the track. Not only does this boost their metabolism throughout the day, but it also helps them to wake up and be more alert.

Keeping the students alert is a major battle that teachers have to face every day. It is hard to keep students motivated for learning, especially in the morning when everyone has just crawled out of bed. Most teachers believe that running laps every day has a definite effect on students’ alertness, focus and also helps to improve their grades greatly. Additionally, doing laps also improves the overall health of students.
questions: How many laps are they required to run?
What are the benefits of exercising before classes start?
What effect does exercise seem to have on grades?
2.translation: 我們的國中十分相信提倡體能運動對學生有益。在每天早上的朝會過後，學生必須跑操場三圈。這不僅會促進他們一天的新陳代謝，也能幫助他們精神振作，並且變得更加靈敏。 讓學生保持靈敏是老師每天必須面對的重大挑戰。特別是早上學生剛從床上爬起來，要讓他們維持學習的動機是很困難的。大多數的老師相信每天跑操場對學生的靈活度與專注力有顯著的效果，並且能夠大大地提昇他們的成績。此外，跑操場也能改善學生整體的健康。
answers: They are required to run 3 laps around the track.
It improves focus, alertness, and helps students to wake up. It also boosts metabolism.
It seems to improve grades greatly.

2.title: Peter and the Drinking Fountain
paras: Peter has taken a break from class. His classroom is always very hot during the summer months and he needs to drink lots of water so that he doesn’t get dehydrated.  He has also been diligently studying all morning in the hot classroom and has worked up a thirst.

The thirsty boy wanders down the long hallway to the nearest water fountain. As he turns the knob on the top of the stainless steel fountain, water begins to stream out. He lets it run for a few seconds to cool it off. He then bends over and takes a sip. Ah, it refreshes him instantly and he continues slurping away.
The cool water quickly quenches his thirst and he wipes his mouth on his sleeve.  Satisfied now, he slowly returns to class, trying to waste as much time as possible.
questions: What had Peter been doing that made him thirsty?
Why does Peter let the water run for a few seconds before he takes a drink?
What does Peter wipe his mouth on?
2.translation: 彼得下課了。他的教室在夏天的這幾個月份都很熱，所以他需要補充很多水分才不至於脫水。他整個早上都在很熱的教室裡用功讀書，使得他很口渴。這個口渴的小男孩緩慢的沿著長廊走到了最近的飲水機。當他轉動不鏽鋼飲水機上面的轉鈕時，水就慢慢的流出來。他讓它流了幾秒，讓水涼一點才喝。然後他彎下腰去喝一小口。啊！這一小口使他立刻恢復精神，接著他繼續咕嚕咕嚕地喝。 那涼爽的水很快地解除他的口渴，然後他用袖子擦擦他的嘴巴。此刻，彼得感到很滿足。他緩慢的走回教室，試著要儘量拖延一些時間。
answers: He had been diligently studying all morning.
He lets the water run to cool it off.
He wipes his mouth on his sleeve.

2.title: Bilingual Signs
paras: Taiwanese schools are trying to become more and more bilingual all the time. A good way to do this is to make all existing signs English and Chinese like exit, bathroom and other common signs. Another way is to put signs in high traffic areas to increase exposure to English.

This particular school has done this by placing old adages on the stairs. It works quite well as students cannot go up the stairs without having to look at the sayings. They may choose not to read them for a while, but chances are after going up and down the steps repeatedly they will eventually read them all out of curiosity.
The signs have English on the left and Chinese on the right. They are also brightly colored to catch your eye.
questions: What is a good way to make schools bilingual?
Where should schools put the signs to increase exposure?
Why are the old adages brightly colored?
2.translation: 台灣的學校一直試著要變得更為雙語化，而將目前所有的標誌做成中英對照是個好方法，例如：出口、洗手間，以及其它常見的標誌。另一個方式是把標誌放在人來人往的地方，以增加學生接觸英文的機會。 這所特定的學校把一些古老的諺語放在階梯來達成雙語化的目的。由於學生上樓梯時一定會看到這些諺語，所以它的效果很好。也許會有一段時間他們選擇不去看這些諺語，但是很可能因為要不斷的上下樓梯，最後只因出於好奇心而去閱讀它們。 這些標誌左邊是英文，右邊是中文。它們的色彩也很鮮明，以便能吸引你的目光。
answers: A good way to do this is by making all existing signs English and Chinese.
They should put them in high traffic areas to increase exposure.
They are brightly colored to catch your eye.

2.title: Eli Makes Instant Noodles
paras: Picture 1 Eli peels back the lid of the Styrofoam container and finds a package of seasoning sitting on top of the instant noodles.
Picture 2 He tears open the seasoning packet for the broth and sprinkles it over the top of the noodles.
Picture 3 Eli then pours boiling water from the kettle over the noodles, filling the container almost to the top.
Picture 4 Next, he re-covers the container with the foil lid, and weighs it down with his chopsticks.  He needs to let it stand until the noodles are nice and soft—about 3 minutes or so.
Picture 5 Now that the noodles are cooked, Eli slowly peels back the foil, careful not to burn himself with the steam.
Picture 6 He lets the noodles sit for an additional minute to cool off before eating them. He certainly seems to be enjoying his Cup O’ Noodles.
questions: What does Eli do with the seasoning?
How hot does the water have to be to make noodles?
Why is Eli careful when he takes the lid off?
2.translation: 照片一伊萊把保麗龍碗的蓋子往後掀開，在泡麵上面看到一包湯頭調味料。 照片二他撕開湯頭調味包，並且將它倒在泡麵上面。 照片三接著，伊萊將茶壺中的沸水倒在泡麵上，水量差不多到碗的最上方。 照片四接下來，他再把錫箔紙蓋蓋上，並且用他的筷子壓住蓋子，他必須靜置大約三分鐘，直到麵條變得Q軟好吃。 照片五現在泡麵已經泡好了，伊萊慢慢地把保麗龍碗的蓋子往後掀開，他小心翼翼地，以免自己被熱氣燙傷。 照片六食用前，他再多等一分鐘，讓泡麵稍微變涼。 毫無疑問的，他似乎很享受他的杯麵。
answers: He sprinkles it over the top of the noodles.
It has to be boiling hot.
He is careful so that he doesn’t burn himself with the steam.

2.title: No Cafeteria is No Problem
paras: Most schools in Taiwan do not have cafeterias. Instead, they hire caterers to do all the cooking and bring it to the schools. The contracted caterers do not prepare the food on-site; they do it in their own kitchens. Once the food is ready, it is packed into containers and dropped off 7at the schools. Each container holds the same thing so that there is no worry of a mix-up as to which one goes where. Representatives from each class then pick up the food at the drop-off point and bring it back to their classrooms.

At our school, the lunch containers are put on chairs or tables at the front of the classroom near the chalkboard. Volunteers then distribute the food evenly amongst the students. If there are any leftovers, students are allowed to have second helpings. As you can see in the second picture, the students here are very hungry and can’t wait to dish up.

Every student carries a small lunch bag that contains utensils and a stainless steel bowl. They pack their lunch bag with them every day, but if they happen to forget, there are disposable bowls and utensils available so that no one has to go hungry. After lunch, each student is responsible for cleaning his or her own dish.
questions: Do most schools in Taiwan have cafeterias?
What is the food packed into so that it can be brought to the schools?
What do the students have to pack every day so that they can eat?
2.translation: 在台灣，大部分的學校都沒有自助餐廳，而是僱用辦理外食的廠商來幫他們料理飯菜，然後再送到學校來。和學校簽約的廠商並非在現場料理食材，而是在他們自己的廚房做。一旦食物做好後，就會被裝在餐桶送到學校。每一個餐桶裡都裝著相同的菜色，所以不必擔心餐桶擺放的位置會搞混。各班接著派代表到放置餐桶的地方，將食物抬回他們的教室。 在我們學校，餐桶放在靠近教室前方黑板的椅子或是桌子上。然後，志願幫忙盛飯菜的學生，便會把食物平均分配給每一位同學。如果分完後還有剩下的話，同學們就可以再取用一次飯菜。就如同您在第二張照片中所看到的，這群學生們都已是飢腸轆轆，迫不及待地要開動了!     每一個學生都會帶一個裝餐具和不鏽鋼碗的小餐袋。他們每天都帶餐袋來學校。不過要是他們忘了帶的話，學校裡也有免洗餐具，所以就用不著擔心會餓肚子了。吃完午餐後，每一位學生都要負責清洗他們自己的餐具。
answers: No, most schools in Taiwan do not have cafeterias.
It is packed into containers.
They have to pack utensils and a bowl.

2.title: Jack Eats Out
paras: Jack and his family are eating out this evening. The place they have chosen is a buffet-style restaurant. These restaurants offer a wide range of foods prepared in many ways.

After taking a plate to put the food on, Jack heads to the steam table. The steam table keeps the food warm even after sitting for some time. Each tray on the table contains a different dish. Jack eyes the tray heaped with stewed tofu. He thinks it looks very good, so he digs right in.

After putting a few vegetables on his plate, he takes the food to the cashier. In this restaurant you are charged by weight. This means you have to pay so much per gram of food. At some buffet-style restaurants you are charged by the number of items and not by weight, so if you want to sample many dishes it may be a lot more expensive.
Included with the price of Jack’s meal are soup and black tea. He takes advantage by repeatedly filling up his bowl with steaming hot soup. He sure loves soup, doesn't he?
Finally, Jack sits down to enjoy his meal. His family is still dishing up, but they will join him soon. By that time Jack will probably be ready for a second helping. Bon appetit, Jack!
questions: Where have Jack and his family chosen to eat at?
What does Jack think looks very good?
Do you have to pay by item at this restaurant?
2.translation: 今天晚上，傑克和他的家人到外面去吃飯。他們所選擇的地點是一間自助式餐廳。這種餐廳提供了很多種以不同方式烹調的食物。 在拿了一個裝食物的盤子後，傑克走向了蒸氣保溫餐桌。這個餐桌可以保持食物的熱度，即使食物已經放了一段時間。桌上每個盤子都裝著不同的菜餚。傑克看著一個裝滿滷豆腐的托盤，他認為這道菜看起來很好吃，所以他立即挖了一勺。 在裝了一些青菜到他的餐盤之後，他端著盤子走到結帳處。這間餐廳是根據食物的重量來算錢的，這意謂著你必須按食物每公克的價格來付費。某些自助式餐廳是根據你吃幾種菜餚來算錢，而不是重量。所以，如果你想要品嚐很多樣的菜餚，那費用可能就會貴很多。 傑克這餐的費用包含了湯和紅茶。他善用這項優惠，將熱湯一碗一碗地盛到碗裡。他真的很喜歡喝湯，不是嗎? 最後，傑克坐下來享用他的餐點。他的家人們還在挑選菜餚，但他們很快就會加入他的行列。到那時候，傑克將已將準備好要吃第二盤了。傑克，祝你胃口大開!
answers: They have chosen to eat at a buffet-style restaurant.
He thinks the tray heaped with stewed tofu looks very good.
No, you have to pay by weight.

2.title: Document Photos
paras: In Taiwan there are a lot of forms that require photos to be attached. The standard size photo is 4 * 5 cm for most forms. They are especially important for passports, visas and hospital records. On such forms there is a box at the top specifically for this purpose.

Picture This girl is removing a photo from a small reddish envelope. She is filling out an application to take a GEPT test. The form requires her picture so that the test administrator can verify her identity when she takes the test. This prevents the girl from having another person take the test for her.

Picture 2 She applies glue to the back of the photo and then sticks it to the application. It is now ready to be mailed and, with any luck, she will be accepted to take the upcoming test; otherwise, she has to wait for the next test.
questions: What do you have to attach to a lot of forms in Taiwan?
What is the girl filling out an application for?
Why does the GEPT form require a photo?
2.translation: 在台灣有很多表格都要求貼上照片。在大多數的表格中，照片的標準尺寸為4*5公分。照片在護照、簽證及醫院記錄上格外重要。在這一類表格的上方會有一個小格子專門用來貼照片。照片一這女孩正從紅色小袋子裡拿出一張照片。她正在填寫參加全民英檢的報名表。這份表格需要貼上她的照片，以便監考人員在考試時能確認她的身份。這是為了防止她請別人代替她考試。照片二她在照片背後塗上膠水，然後把照片黏在報名表上。現在報名表準備要寄出去了。若一切順利，她的報名即將被受理，然後她便能參加最近的這次考試。不然，她就得等到下一梯次的測驗了。
answers: You have to attach a photo.
She is filling out an application for a GEPT test.
So that the test administrator can verify the girl’s identity when she takes the test.

2.title: Henry’s Vanity
paras: Henry is quite fond of himself. He would stand in front of the mirror all day if his mother would allow it. He likes to try on many different outfits in his wardrobe and model them in front of the mirror, too. Sometimes, he even pretends he is on the catwalk modeling the latest fashions for all the designers to see.

Whatever his motivations are, his mother is disturbed by his vain performances and has removed all the mirrors and reflective surfaces from the house—even the toaster had to go. Now, if he wants to see himself, he has to fill the bathtub with water and stare at his reflection.
questions: What does Henry like to stand in front of?
How does Henry’s mother feel about him doing this?
What has she done to prevent him from staring at himself?
2.translation: 亨利有點自戀。假如他的媽媽允許的話，他可能會一整天都站在鏡子前。他喜歡試穿衣櫥裡各種不同的服飾，然後在鏡子前展示一番。有時候，他甚至假裝自己正站在伸展台上，為所有的設計師展現最新流行的服飾。 不管他的動機為何，他如此愛慕虛榮的行徑，令他的媽媽困擾不已。她已經把家中所有表面可以反射的物品通通拿走，甚至連烤麵包機也一樣。現在，如果他想看到自己，他必須先將浴缸裝滿水，好凝視自己水中的倒影。
answers: He likes to stand in front of the mirror.
She is disturbed by it.
She has removed all the mirrors and reflective surfaces from the house.

2.title: Bulletin Boards
paras: Schools often use main entrances to post announcements for students and parents to read. This school is no exception as they have put up a number of bulletin boards along the main hallway. To prevent important notices from being removed or vandalized, they have covered the bulletin boards with protective sheets of glass.

Some things you may find on the announcement boards are class schedules, upcoming events, student awards, or notices to remind students of important test dates. Diligent students always check the board to make sure that they know what's going on at their school. The school staff is responsible for periodically updating the boards with current information and removing anything that is outdated, so that the information posted is always current and accurate.
questions: Where do schools usually put bulletin boards?
Why are the bulletin boards covered with glass?
Who is responsible for updating the bulletin boards?
2.translation: 學校經常利用主要的入口張貼公告給學生和家長閱讀。這所學校也不例外，因為他們沿著川堂擺設一些佈告欄。為了防止重要的告示被移除或破壞，他們用防護的玻璃窗來遮住佈告欄。 一些你可以在佈告欄看到的訊息有班級課表、即將舉辦的活動、學生獎勵，或提醒學生重要考試日期的告示。用功的學生總會去查看佈告欄，以確認他們知道學校發生的事情。學校的職員負責定期更新佈告欄，他們張貼當前的資訊，並移除過期的資料，如此一來，張貼在上面的資訊就會一直保持是最新而且是正確的。
answers: They usually put bulletin boards in the main entrance.
They are covered with glass to prevent important notices from being removed or vandalized.
The school staff is responsible for updating the bulletin boards.

2.title: Bottled Water
paras: When you are on the go, the best option to re-hydrate yourself is bottled water. It is readily available at most stores and there are countless brands and types of water to choose from. The number of choices is almost mind-boggling. You have distilled water, spring water, carbonated water and flavored water—just to name a few. Whatever their name, they all serve the same purpose: quenching your thirst.

The lady pictured here has just unscrewed the lid on her bottle of water. When buying bottled water, make sure the safety seal is not broken, so that you can be sure that the water hasn’t been tampered with and is fresh. If the seal is broken, it is best not to drink it just to be on the safe side.

In the second picture, our beautiful young model is taking a drink. She tips back the bottle and starts guzzling it down like there’s no tomorrow. She seems to be very thirsty. Perhaps she was lost in the desert and was unable to find water to quench her thirst. After finishing, she tosses the empty bottle into the recycling bin.
questions: How can you be sure that your bottle of water is safe to drink
Do you have a favorite kind of water that you like to drink?
What does the lady do with the empty bottle?

vocab.w: to be on the safe side

2.translation: 當你外出時，補充水分最好的選擇就是瓶裝水。你可以輕易地在大部分商店買到瓶裝水，而且有非常多的品牌及種類可以買，可供選擇的數量多到幾乎令人吃驚。你可以選擇蒸餾水、礦泉水、汽水，及加味水等等，這只是其中的一些種類而已。不管它們的名稱是什麼，他們要達成的目的都是一樣的：解渴。 照片中的女士，剛剛把她的瓶裝水的瓶蓋打開。當購買瓶裝水時，要確定安全封口是完好無缺的，如此一來，你才有把握這瓶水沒有被蓄意毀損，而且是全新的。如果安全封口有破損，為了安全著想，最好不要去喝它。 在第二張圖當中，我們這位年輕貌美的模特兒正在喝水。她把瓶子拿斜並且開始狂飲，好像沒有明天似的。她似乎感到非常口渴。也許她在沙漠中迷路，無法找到水來解渴。喝完水後，她將空瓶丟到資源回收桶裡。
answers: By checking to see if the safety seal is broken.
Yes, I like lemon-flavored water.
She puts it into the recycling bin. She recycles it.

3.title: How to Make a Scarecrow
paras: This is a scarecrow. It is standing alone in a field. Scarecrows are used to scare away birds that may eat crops. They are supposed to look like people standing in the field. If the birds think the field is inhabited, they will probably not stop there.

To make a scarecrow all you need are a couple of poles, some straw and an old shirt. First, you make a cross out of the poles and tie them together. Then you put a shirt on the cross and stuff it with straw. Next you need something that looks like a head to put on the top. Finally, put a hat on the head and you’re finished. Now all you have to do is to stick it in the ground in your field or garden.
Without scarecrows, some farmers may resort to shooting or poisoning birds. In reality, scarecrows save crops and birds. Everyone is a winner.
questions: Why do farmers put scarecrows up?
What does a scarecrow look like to a bird?
What things do you need to make a scarecrow?
3.translation: 這是一個稻草人，它獨自地站在稻田裡。稻草人被用來嚇走可能會吃農作物的鳥類。它們看起來應該要像是有人站在田裡的樣子。如果鳥類以為田裡有人居住，它們可能就不會在那裡停留。 做一個稻草人只需要兩根竿子、一些乾草和一件舊襯衫。首先，用竿子做成一個十字架，並且把它們綁起來。然後把襯衫穿在十字架上面，並在裡面塞進乾草。接下來，你需要將一個看起來像頭部的東西放在頂端。最後，放一頂帽子在頭上就大功告成了。現在，你只需將它插在你田裡或菜園的土裡即可。 不用稻草人的話，有些農夫可能會用射殺或毒害小鳥的方式。事實上，稻草人拯救了農作物和鳥類。每個人都是贏家。
answers: They put them up to scare away birds.
It looks like a person standing in the field.
You need a couple of poles, some straw, an old shirt, a hat and something that looks like a head.

3.title: The Claw Game
paras: Luke is trying to win a stuffed toy for his daughter. To operate the claw vending machine, 3 he first needs to insert a coin. When he hears the music start, it's time for action. He uses the joystick to maneuver the claw forward, backward, left and right. When the claw is positioned over top of the toy, he hits the button on the right. The open claw then drops down and attempts to grab a prize. At this point the claw, and Luke’s prize, begin to return to the top of the machine. Usually when the grapple hook gets to the top, it drops the item and ends your turn, but not this time. The claw has a firm hold on the stuffed toy, it then moves toward the hole on the left side. Luke is excited because he has never had a toy make it this far. The claw slowly opens and drops the prize into the hole. Luke reaches in and proudly claims his prize. More often than not, people walk away without a toy and without any change left in their pockets, but on this day, Luke was one of the lucky ones.
questions: How does Luke know when his turn starts?
What does he use to move the claw around?
What usually happens when the claw gets to the top?
3.translation: 路克正試著為他的女兒贏得一個填充玩具。要操作夾娃娃機，首先他必須投入一枚硬幣。當他聽到音樂啟動時，就是開始行動的時候了。他用搖桿操作機器夾，讓它前、後、左、右的移動。當機器夾移動到玩具上方的位置時，他按下右邊的按鈕。這時，張開的機器夾會下降，並且試著抓住獎品。在這個時候，機器夾和路克的獎品開始回到機器的上方。通常當抓鉤到達頂端時，抓住的東西會掉下來，遊戲就結束了，但路克這次並沒有這樣，機器夾牢牢地抓住了填充玩具，並且移動到左邊的洞口。他非常的興奮，因為他從未把玩具夾到這麼遠。機器夾慢慢地張開，把獎品投到洞口。路克把手伸進去，驕傲地取出他的獎品。人們多半在離開時沒有夾到玩具，而且口袋裡的零錢也花光了。但是今天路克是屬於幸運的一員。
answers: He knows because he hears music.
He uses a joystick to move the claw around.
It usually drops your prize.

3.title: Correcting Your Mistakes
paras: When making study notes, it is a good idea to keep them neat and legible. Sloppy notes will only lead to confusion and lost time when you study. Try to use pencil as much as possible because it is easier to correct mistakes and then when you do mess up, you can simply erase the mistake and write it again. This will help to keep your work nice and neat.

If you must work with a pen, or are working with photocopied materials, using an eraser is not an option. In these cases you must use either correction fluid or correction tape for your mistakes. I find correction tape the tidiest and you can write over it almost as soon as it is applied, too. Correction fluid is okay as well, but it will require drying time and doesn’t look quite as nice. Sometimes dried bits of liquid paper end up on your work, making it difficult to write neatly. But in a pinch, I suppose, it is better than nothing.
questions: How should your study notes look?
What should you do with your mistakes?
Which is tidier, correction tape or correction fluid?
3.translation: 當你在做筆記時，保持字跡工整與易讀是很好的。當你在讀書時，筆記的字跡潦草只會導致困惑，而且浪費時間。儘量使用鉛筆，因為錯誤比較容易修正，而且當你的筆記弄亂時，你可以簡單地擦掉錯誤並且重寫。這將有助於使你的筆記非常整齊。 假如你必須用原子筆，或是處理影印的資料，你是不能選擇使用橡皮擦的。在這種情形下，你必須使用修正液或修正帶來處理你的錯誤。我覺得使用修正帶是最整潔的方式，而且你還可以在用了修正帶後，馬上寫上去。修正液也可以使用，但是它需要變乾的時間，而且看起來不像用修正帶那樣地好看。有時候修正液乾掉後形成的小紙塊讓你更難寫得整齊。但我想在緊急情況時，這總比什麼都沒有要來得好。
answers: They should look neat and legible.
You should correct/erase/change them.
Correction tape is tidier.

3.title: A Pocketful of Change
paras: Paul’s pocket was heavy with change after a day of shopping. He reached his hand into his pocket and grabbed handful after handful of change, placing it on the coffee table. Sitting on the comfy armchair, he began to stack the change. He put the one dollar coins in stacks of 10 to make them easier to count. After he sorted them all out, he added them up. He had nearly 200 dollars in change. It was no wonder his pocket felt so heavy. He took the change and put it in his piggybank to save it for a day when he would want to buy something.
questions: Why was Paul’s pocket so heavy?
Why did he put them in stacks of 10?
Where did he put the change after he counted it?
3.translation: 購物了一整天之後，保羅的口袋因為裝零錢變得很重。他把手伸進他的口袋，然後拿出一把一把的零錢放在咖啡桌上。他坐在舒適的扶手椅上，開始把硬幣疊起來。他把一元的硬幣十個十個疊成堆，這樣比較容易數。在他把全部的硬幣分類好之後，他計算了零錢的總數。他的零錢將近有兩百元，難怪他的口袋感覺這麼重。他把零錢放入撲滿存起來，以便改天想買東西時就可以拿來用。
answers: It was heavy because it was full of change.
He put them in stacks of 10 to make them easier to count.
He put it in his piggybank.

3.title: The Dish Dryer
paras: Lester has just finished washing the dishes. After he washes the dishes, he places each of them into the dish-dryer above the sink. The wire rack in the dryer holds plates and bowls on their side. This allows air to flow freely between the dishes as they dry. It also helps to organize the dishes so that you can fit more of them into the dryer. There is a spot on the side for utensils, too. It holds them upright, which allows water to drip off of them.

When the machine is fully loaded, he can turn it on, but he must close the door first. The glass door slides out from above and then swings down to cover the front of the unit. To turn it on, he needs to select the proper cycle and push the button. The machine will then dry the dishes with hot air.
The whole process takes about ten minutes or so. For the sake of energy conservation, you should try to make sure the machine is full before you turn it on.
questions: How does Lester place the dishes in the dish rack?
Why are the dishes placed on their side?
How long does the whole process take?
3.translation: 列斯特剛把碗盤洗好。他洗完之後，把碗盤一一的放到洗滌槽上面的烘碗機裡。烘碗機裡的金屬架可以讓碗盤側放，這樣一來，在烘乾的時候，空氣可以不受阻礙地在碗盤之間流動。這種方式也能將碗盤排放整齊，讓你能放更多的碗盤到烘碗機裡。在烘碗機的邊緣還有個放餐具的位置，餐具放得直直的，好讓水可以滴下來。 當烘碗機裝滿碗盤之後，他就可以打開電源了。但是在啟動之前，他得先把門關上才行。玻璃門從上方滑出，然後往下擺動蓋住烘碗機的前面。要啟動時，他必須選擇適合的循環模式，並且按下按鈕，機器便會開始用熱空氣來烘乾碗盤了。整個過程大概會花十分鐘左右。為了節約能源起見，在啟動之前，你應該先確認烘碗機是否有擺滿碗盤。
answers: He places them in on their side/edge.
They are placed on their side to allow the air to flow freely through them.
It takes about 10 minutes or so.

3.title: Andre Makes a Scrapbook
paras: Hi, my name is Andre and I love collecting basketball memorabilia. I collect pins, hats, stickers, shirts, banners and anything I can get my hands on. In my spare time I have also made a scrapbook.

My favorite team has always been the Denver Nuggets, so the bulk of the book is of them. Every day I look through the sports sections of the local and national papers. If I find an interesting article about a Nuggets’ player, I cut it outand put it in my scrapbook. There are pages and pages of such newspaper clippings in my book.

As long as I can remember, Allen Iverson has been my favorite player. I actually have another scrapbook just for him. Inside this book there are pictures that I have collected from various magazines and papers. They span his entire career. I have pictures of his first pro game, his first all-star game, and even some of his personal life. As well, every milestone he has passed is documented in my book. I think I must be his biggest fan.

When I am old, I will be able to look back through my scrapbooks and remember my heroes in all their glory. Hopefully, my children will want to look at them, too. Maybe it will inspire them to start a scrapbook of their own.
questions: What kinds of things does Andre like to collect?
What does Andre do in his spare time?
Have you ever made a scrapbook about something or someone?
3.translation: 嗨，我叫做安德烈。我喜歡收集一些籃球紀念品。我會收集胸針、帽子、貼紙、運動衫、旗子，以及任何我可以得到的東西。在我空閒的時候，我也製作了一本剪貼簿。 我最喜愛的球隊一直都是丹佛金塊隊，所以我剪貼簿的資料大部分都是關於他們的。我每天都會瀏覽地方與全國性報紙的體育版，如果我看到一篇關於金塊隊球員有趣的文章，我都會把它剪下來貼到我的剪貼簿上。我的本子裡有一頁頁像這樣子的新聞剪報。 自從我有記憶以來，艾倫‧艾弗森一直是我最喜愛的球員。事實上，我有另一本他專屬的剪貼簿。在這本簿子裡，有我從不同的雜誌和報紙收集來的照片。這些照片涵蓋了他整個籃球生涯。我有他初次在職籃比賽的照片，他第一場明星賽的照片，甚至一些個人的生活照。此外，他所經歷過的所有重要事件都記錄在我的本子裡。我想我一定是他的頭號粉絲。 當我老的時候，我會去回顧我的剪貼簿，並回想我那些處於光榮時期的英雄。但願我的小孩也會想要看看他們，這也許能激發他們開始製作自己的剪貼簿呢！
answers: He collects basketball pins, banners, hats, stickers and shirts (memoribilia).
He makes scrapbooks.
Yes, I have made a scrapbook about a professional baseball player.

3.title: Posing for a Picture
paras: At one of the local markets, an artist has set up a shop. There are examples of his work hung on the walls for you to admire. If you want, he will draw your picture, too.

Posing for a picture will require that you sit very still. If you move around too much, it will be difficult for the artist to sketch your picture. As you sit, he will study the lines and shape of your face, and then capture them on paper. He will try to make you as beautiful as possible by enhancing your good features and maybe overlooking some of the bad ones. Don’t expect miracles, though, as there is only so much he can hide.

The whole process will take about 20 minutes or so. When he has finished, you will have immortalized yourself in a piece of art and can hang it on a wall in your house for all to see. Don’t buy too many pictures of yourself, though, as your family might get sick of looking at you on every wall in the house.
questions: Why does he hang examples of his work on the wall?
What do you have to do if you pose for a picture?
How long does the process take?
3.translation: 在當地的一個市場裡，有一位藝術家設立一間工作室。牆上掛著許多他作品的樣本供你欣賞。如果你願意的話，他也可以為你畫一張畫像。 擺姿勢畫像時，你必須端坐不動。如果你動得太厲害，畫家很難為你畫一張素描。當你坐著時，他會仔細地觀察你臉上的線條及輪廓，然後將這些特點描繪在紙上。畫家會試著加強你的優點，也許也會忽略一些缺點，盡可能讓你看起來美麗動人。然而，別期望奇蹟的出現，因為他能掩飾的就只有這麼多。 整個素描的過程需要花費約二十分鐘左右。當他完成時，你將得以永恆不朽地活在藝術品中，而且可以把畫像掛在家中的牆上供大家欣賞。然而，千萬不要買太多自己的畫像，若是每一面牆都會看到你的畫像，你的家人也許會感到很厭煩。
answers: He hangs them on the wall for people to admire and for advertising purposes.
You have to sit very still.
It takes about 20 minutes or so.

3.title: Recycling Containers
paras: Three red bins and a yellow basket are sitting along the wall in the back of a classroom. They are filled with recyclable materials, except for one which is used for garbage. They are part of the school’s recycling program.

Each bin and the basket holds something different. One is used for juice boxes and tetra packs. One is used for plastics, such as tea glasses and plastic bottles. The third holds regular garbage and the yellow basket is used for paper and cardboard.

Recycling waste is a good way to conserve valuable resources. Instead of cutting down more trees to make new paper, we can recycle waste paper and use it again. All sorts of things can be made from recycled materials. It also helps to keep landfill sites from overflowing by reducing the amount of garbage stored in them.

Here students are sorting the bins. Any garbage is removed and items are organized and bagged according to what they are and what can be recycled with them. They will then be taken to a recycling depot.
questions: What are the bins in the back of the class used for?
What is in the yellow basket?
Do you recycle?
3.translation: 教室後面的牆邊擺著三個紅色的桶子和一個黃色的籃子。他們裡面裝滿了回收的物品，除了一個是被用來裝垃圾的之外。這些是學校資源回收計畫的一部分。 每個桶子和籃子分別用來放不同的物品。一個用來裝果汁盒和利樂包；一個裝塑膠製品，像是茶杯和塑膠瓶；第三個桶子裝一般垃圾，而黃色的籃子則是用來放紙張和紙板的。 垃圾回收是保存珍貴資源的好方法。我們可以回收廢紙再度使用，而不是砍伐更多的樹木來製造新的紙張。回收的材料能做成各式各樣的東西，而且資源回收可以減少囤積在掩埋場的垃圾量，避免掩埋場的垃圾多到滿出來。 這裡有一群學生正把桶子內的東西加以分類。他們去除垃圾，再根據東西的種類以及可以回收的部分來處理裝袋。然後這些東西就被送到資源回收的地點。
answers: They are used for recycling.
Paper and cardboard are in it.
Yes, because I care about the environment.

3.title: Back It Up!
paras: The computer prompt tells Alice to please insert a disc into the CD-ROM drive. She pushes the eject button and the tray slides out automatically. She puts the CD on the tray and gently pushes it in. The tray then slides back in and she can hear the disc begin to whirl as the computer reads it.

Alice has also saved some of her homework as a Word file on her memory stick. It is like a small portable hard drive. All you have to do to use it is to plug it into an unused USB port. Once connected, there will be an icon in “my computer” or on your taskbar that will open it in a separate window. You can then use the file as you normally would on your own computer.

Portable memory devices are a very convenient way of carrying your files around without having to burn them to CD, but they are not one-hundred percent reliable. With this in mind, you should always back up important documents or files somewhere else.
questions: What tells Alice to put in the CD?
What does she hear when the tray goes back in?
Where do you plug in a flash drive on your computer?
3.translation: 電腦出現一個提示，要求愛莉絲將光碟放到光碟機。她按下退出鍵，托盤就自動滑出來了。她把光碟片放到托盤上面，並輕輕的將它推入。當托盤滑回去後，她可以聽到電腦讀取光碟片時，片子在旋轉的聲音。 愛莉絲也將一些家庭作業以Word檔存在她的隨身碟裡。隨身碟就像一種小型的可攜式硬碟。如果你要使用它，只需要將它插入未使用的USB孔中。一旦連結了，會有一個圖像出現在「我的電腦」或者在工作列上，然後有另一個視窗開啟隨身碟的內容。如此一來，你就可以使用隨身碟的檔案，就如同你平常在使用電腦上的檔案一樣。 可攜式的記憶裝置可讓你方便地攜帶檔案，而不用燒錄到光碟片。但是它們並非百分之百可靠。考慮到這個因素，你應該經常把重要的文件或檔案備份到其它的地方。
answers: The computer prompt tells Alice to put in a CD.
She hears the disc spinning.
You plug it into a USB port.

3.title: Water Dispensers
paras: This is a coin-operated water dispenser. Because the water in Taiwan is not safe to use for drinking or cooking, many people rely on these machines for their potable water. They are a cheap alternative to buying bottled water.

These types of water dispensers are very simple to use. You only need to bring a suitable container and some change. Then you insert a coin into the slot on the machine, put the nozzle into your container and open the valve. Be sure your container is safe for potable water and that you use it only for this purpose.

When you are finished filling your container, turn off the valve and place the nozzle back into the holder. Hopefully you haven’t bought more water than you can carry home or you might have to drink it until it is light enough to carry.
questions: Why do people in Taiwan use water dispensers?
What are some other alternatives to using a water machine?
Where does the water in the machine come from?
3.translation: 這是一個投幣式的供水器。因為台灣的水用來喝或烹調並不安全，所以很多人依賴這些機器來取得他們的飲用水。和購買瓶裝水相較之下，它們是便宜的替代品。 這類型的供水器很容易操作。你只需要帶一個適當的容器和一些零錢，然後將硬幣投入機器的投幣孔裡，接下來將噴嘴放入你的容器中，再打開活門即可。請確認你用來裝飲用水的容器是合乎安全的，而且絕對只能用在裝飲用水的這個用途。 當水裝滿容器時，關閉活門，並將噴嘴放回吊掛處。但願你買的水不會超過你能夠提回去的量，否則你可能必須喝掉一些，直到桶子輕到可以讓你提回去為止。
answers: They use water dispensers because the water here is not safe for drinking or cooking.
You can buy bottled water, use spring water, or boil tap water.
It comes from the mountains on a water truck.

4.title: Mailing My Friend
paras: I haven’t seen my friend Kelly in a long time. She moved away to Pingdong three years ago because her father got a new job there. Since then, the only contact we’ve had is by mail. I really miss her.

This week I wrote her a very long letter to catch up on everything that has been going on. As always, I took the letter down to the post office to be mailed. There are two mailboxes outside the main building to use. The red drop-off box has two slots, one for express mail and the other for airmail. I don’t use either because they cost more for postage than regular mail. The green mailbox is for regular mail and it also has two slots. The slot on the right is for local mail. The one on the left is for anything outside this township.

Every time I drop a letter into the mailbox, I wonder how long it will be until my friend replies. Usually she writes back quickly, but if she is busy like I sometimes am, it can take a while. I hope it isn’t so long this time because I miss her so much.
questions: Why hasn’t the girl seen her friend in a long time?
What is the only contact the two girls have?
How many mailboxes are there outside the post office?
4.translation: 我很久沒有看到我的朋友凱莉了。她三年前搬到屏東，因為她的父親在那裡找到了一份新的工作。自從那時候開始，我們唯一的聯絡方式就是信件了。我真的很想念她。 這星期我寫了一封很長的信給她，聯繫最近發生的每件事情。一如往常，我把信拿到郵局去寄。郵局外有兩個郵筒可以使用。紅色的郵筒有兩個投遞孔，一個是限時郵件的投遞孔，另一個則是寄航空郵件專用的。我沒有使用任何其中一個，因為它們的郵資比起普通郵件還要來得貴。綠色的郵筒是寄普通郵件用的，它也有兩個投遞孔。右邊的用來寄當地郵件，而左邊的則是寄到外地的投遞孔。 每當我投遞信件時，我都很想知道我的朋友多久會回信。通常，她回信的速度很快；不過如果她很忙的話，就如同我有時候也會很忙一樣，就會花上一段時間了。我希望這次不會太久，因為我真的很想念她。
answers: Because her friend moved to Ping Dong.
The only contact the two girls have is by mail.
There are two mailboxes there.

4.title: Withdrawing Money
paras: I recently needed to withdraw some money from my account at the post office. I went there with my passbook and chop. On my way in, I grabbed a withdrawal slip and found an empty spot at the counter.

I filled in the required information on the slip, such as the account number, my name and the amount that I wished to take out. I wrote neatly and carefully so that the teller would have no trouble reading it. Instead of using my signature, I used a chop. I lightly dabbed the stamp on the red inkpad and then pressed it onto the form.
I must have stood in line for about five minutes, waiting to see the teller at the service counter. When it was finally my turn, I handed her my passbook and the withdrawal slip.

She smiled as she opened the passbook and asked me to key in my password. She then typed a few numbers into her computer and placed my passbook into the printer to be updated. Next, she counted out my money and returned my updated passbook to me.
I thanked the teller for her help. Then I placed the money in my wallet. With the transaction completed, I happily walked out of the post office and went home.
questions: What did I take with me so that I could withdraw money?
What did I use instead of a signature?
4.translation: 最近，我需要從郵局的帳戶裡提領一些錢出來，所以，我帶了我的存摺和印章到郵局去。進去的時候，我拿了一張提款單，並且在櫃台找到一個空位。 我在提款單上面填寫必填的資料，例如：我的帳號、姓名以及我想要提領的金額。我仔細、工整地填寫，好讓行員能看懂我的字跡。我使用印章，而不是用簽名的方式。我把印章輕輕地在紅色印台上沾了一下，然後蓋在提款單上。 我站著排隊要見櫃台的行員，這段時間想必等了五分鐘左右。最後終於輪到我的時候，我把存摺及提款單交給她。 當她打開存摺時，對我笑了一下，並且要求我鍵入我的密碼。接著她在電腦上輸入一些數字，並且將我的存摺放進印表機裡更新資料。接下來，她數一數我領的金額，然後把更新過的存摺還給我。 我感謝這位行員的幫忙，接著把錢放進我的皮夾裡。交易完成後，我高興地走出郵局，然後回家。
answers: You took your passbook and chop with you.
You used your chop.
You had to key in your password.

4.title: Sore Eyes
paras: Cara spent the morning walking around Tienwei for exercise. At the time, a lot of farmers were burning the stubble left over in the rice fields after the harvest. Thick black smoke hung in the morning air and made it a little difficult to breathe and after spending an extended period of time strolling around in the lingering haze, Cara’s eyes became dry, irritated, and bloodshot, so she returned home. Her eyes have been bothering her ever since.

Cara used some eye drops to soothe her sore eyes. First, she twisted the top off the small bottle and double-checked the directions to make sure she was using it properly—she didn’t want to take any chances with her eyesight. She then tilted her head back and carefully pulled her bottom eyelid open. She tried to keep from blinking by staring away from the bottle towards the roof.
Gently, she squeezed the bottle and a drop of the liquid fell onto her bloodshot eye. It began to feel cool and immediately felt better, so she repeated the process with the other eye.

Although her eyes remained bloodshot and puffy for a couple of hours afterwards, by the next day they had completely recovered. The eye drops definitely helped to speed up the healing process. Without them, she might have had sore eyes for a few days.
questions: What was going on in Tienwei while Cara was walking?
What did the smoke do to Cara’s eyes?
What did she do to make sure she used the eye drops properly?
4.translation: 卡拉上午的時候在田尾散步運動。那時有很多農夫正在燒稻子收割後所留下的殘株，造成早晨的空氣中瀰漫著黑色的濃煙，令人呼吸困難。在揮之不去的煙霧中走了一段期間之後，卡拉的眼睛變得乾燥、過敏，而且充滿血絲，所以她就回家了。從那時候起，她的眼睛就一直很不舒服。 卡拉點了一些眼藥水來減輕眼睛的疼痛。首先，她將小瓶子的蓋子轉開，並且仔細地閱讀用藥說明，以確定自己是否有正確地使用藥品─她可不想拿她的視力開玩笑。然後她將頭後仰，小心翼翼地將下眼瞼撥開。為了避免眨眼，她望著天花板，而不去看眼藥水的瓶子。 她輕輕地擠壓瓶身，一滴藥水就滴進了充滿血絲的眼睛。她的眼睛開始覺得涼涼的，而且馬上覺得好多了，所以她就重複這個步驟，點了另外一隻眼睛。 雖然之後的幾個小時她的眼睛依然充滿血絲而且腫腫的，但是到了隔天就完全康復了。眼藥水顯然有助於加速整個復原的過程。如果沒有眼藥水，她的眼睛可能會痛上個好幾天。
answers: A lot of farmers were burning leftover stubble in the rice fields.
It made them dry, irritated and bloodshot.
She double-checked the directions.

4.title: At the Gas Station
paras: Picture 1 A scooter has just pulled into the gas station to fuel up. The attendant asks the customer what she wants. The customer asks her to fill it up with 95 octane fuel. The attendant then grabs the nozzle and turns the pump on.
Picture 2 The customer opens the lid that covers the fuel cap. Then she twists off the cap. She twists very hard because the cap is on tight.

Picture 3 The attendant inserts the nozzle and pulls the trigger. Gas begins to fill the tank and the smelly, flammable fumes rise into the air. She is careful not to pump it out too fast or it might splash out onto the ground.

Picture 4 So that it is easier to make change, the attendant tries to round off the price to a multiple of ten. After finishing, she hangs the nozzle back on the pump and the patron hands her some money. She leaves to put the money in the till and returns with some change and a receipt. The satisfied customer then takes off with a full tank of gas.
questions: What kind of fuel does the customer want?
What does the customer have to do before the attendant can fill up the scooter?
Where does she hang up the nozzle when she is finished with it?
4.translation: 照片一一輛摩托車剛剛騎進了加油站來加油。服務員問這位顧客要加什麼油，顧客請她加滿95無鉛汽油，然後服務員就拿起了加油槍，並啟動了加油幫浦。 照片二顧客打開蓋在油箱蓋子上面的蓋子，然後轉開油箱的蓋子。因為蓋子很緊，她非常用力的轉。 照片三服務員把加油槍插進去，並且啟動加油裝置。汽油開始注入油箱，而那難聞、易燃的的汽油味也飄溢在空氣中。服務員很小心地避免油加得太快，否則汽油很可能會濺灑到地面上。 照片四為了方便找零，服務員試著將油錢湊成10的倍數。加好了之後，服務員將加油槍掛回幫浦。接著顧客遞給她一些錢，她暫時離開將錢放到收銀機內，然後帶著一些零錢和收據走了回來。滿意的顧客搭載著滿滿的汽油啟程了。
answers: The customer wants 95 octane fuel.
The customer has to open the lid that covers the fuel cap and twist off the cap.
She hangs it up on the pump.

4.title: Crossing Guards
paras: With so much traffic on the streets of Taiwan, safety becomes an issue whenever children are going home after school. Because of this, schools have appointed some students to be crossing guards. This is to make it a lot safer for everyone.

The idea is to have the crossing guards stop the oncoming traffic to let cyclists and walkers cross the streets safely. You can tell who the crossing guards are because they usually wear reflective orange vests and may carry a red flag. For added protection, some of them wear helmets in addition to the safety vests.

The red flag tells students that it is not safe to proceed. All the students then line up behind it and wait. When it is safe, the crossing guard will raise the flag and blow a whistle. Students are then permitted to go.

A long pole with brightly colored flags attached to it is used to stop cars. It is very long and can be held across an entire lane of traffic. When the pole is held horizontally across the lane, motorists must stop and wait until the crossing guards let all the students pass. Once it is clear, they will raise the pole vertically again.
The flashing light and pylons, or traffic cones, indicate where the cyclists can ride and they also make the crossing area more visible to motorists. It is another way to make the area safer.

If you are driving in an area such as this, make sure you slow down and watch out for students crossing the road. Stop if the crossing guard indicates that you should do so. This will make it safer for everyone, especially the children.
questions: What have schools appointed some students to be to help solve the problem?
What do the crossing guards have to wear?
Why should you slow down near schools?
4.translation: 由於台灣的街道車輛很多，每當學生放學回家時，安全便成為一個問題。因此，學校指派一些學生來當導護生，這樣可以讓每個人更加安全。 這個構想是要導護生擋下接近中的車輛，好讓騎腳踏車和走路的學生能安全過馬路。你可以分辨出誰是導護生，因為他們通常穿著會反光的橘色背心，並且可能拿著紅色的旗子。為了多一層保護，有些除了穿安全背心之外，還會戴上安全帽。 紅色旗子的作用是要告訴學生現在通行不安全。所有的學生會在旗子後面排隊等候。當安全時，導護生會舉起旗子並吹哨子，允許學生通行。 導護生用一枝長竿來攔住車子，竿子上繫著色彩鮮明的旗子。竹竿很長，可以涵蓋整個車道。當竿子被平握橫越車道時，汽車駕駛必須要停下來，並且等候導護生讓所有的學生通過馬路。一旦學生都走完了，他們會再把竹竿垂直的舉起。 閃示燈和路標(或稱為錐形的交通路標)用來表示腳踏車可以騎的地方，它們也能讓駕駛更清楚地看見馬路穿越區；這是讓這個區域更為安全的另一個方法。 如果你的車子行經這類的地方，一定要減速慢行，並且注意正在過馬路的學生。如果導護生指示你該停車就要停下來，這會讓每個人都更安全，特別是學生們。
answers: They have appointed some students to be crossing guards.
They have to wear reflective vests and helmets.
You should slow down so that you can watch out for students crossing the road.

4.title: The Lion Dance
paras: The Lion Dance has been around for a long time. To keep the tradition alive, it is important to expose children to it. This group of dancers does just that. They travel around Taiwan performing the dance at elementary schools.

The big lion is the main character in the dance. A single man wears the colorful head and another man acts as the hind legs. The two halves of the great animal are connected with red silk cloth that flows nicely as the dancers move around. Most of the children love to watch the long lion frolicking about, but some of the smaller children are frightened of it.

Traditionally the dance is a one-lion dance, but over the years changes have been made in some regions. Sometimes smaller one-man lions accompany the larger one. Perhaps it adds to the excitement of the show to help entertain the children.
questions: What is important to do in order to keep the tradition alive?
Where does this dance group perform at?
Do all children like the lion dance?
4.translation: 舞獅已經有一段很長的歷史。為了使這項傳統技藝得以延續，讓小孩子接觸舞獅表演是很重要的，而這個舞獅團體的成員就是在做這件事，他們到全台各地的小學巡迴演出。 這隻大獅子是表演的主角。一個人佩戴色彩繽紛的獅頭，而另一個人則扮演獅子的後腳。大獅子前後的兩個部分以紅色的絲布連結起來，當他們在舞獅時，絲布流暢地舞動著。大部分的小孩子都喜歡觀看這隻長獅子嬉戲的樣子，但有些年紀較小的孩子則會感到害怕。 傳統的舞獅由一人演出，但是隨著時代演進，某些地區作了一些改變。有時候會有由一人扮演的小獅子來陪襯大獅子，或許這樣可以增加表演刺激的程度，以幫助達到娛樂兒童的目的。
answers: It is important to expose children to it.
They perform at elementary schools.
No, some of them are frightened of it.

4.title: A ‘Fruity’ Gift
paras: Wondering what to buy when you visit your friends? Maybe a box of fruit is just the thing you need. Popular fruits for gift giving include apples, pears, peaches and many others. Whatever is in season is probably the best, but that’s up to you. However, I personally wouldn’t recommend durian, as the smell isn’t so festive and they are just so darned prickly.

In Taiwan, a box of fruit is a very common gift for almost every occasion. Most fruit vendors can make fruit gift boxes. Just tell them what you want and how much you want to spend. They’ll do the rest.

A molded tray is placed on the bottom of the box. This prevents the fruit from moving around and being damaged before it gets to its intended location (no one wants a box of bruised or squashed fruit). The lid is then secured in place by applying some tape, so that nothing falls out. You are now ready to go!

The next time you have to give someone a present, why not make it a box of fruit? Fruit is healthy and relatively inexpensive. It may not be the perfect gift for everyone, but most people will appreciate it because everybody loves fruit.
questions: What is a common gift in Taiwan?
What kind of fruit is best?
What does the molded tray do?
4.translation: 拜訪朋友時，是否在想不知該買什麼東西？也許一盒水果就能符合你的需求。通常用來送禮的水果包括蘋果、梨子、水蜜桃等等。送當季生產的水果也許最好，不過決定權在你。然而，我個人建議不要送榴槤，因為它的味道不太像有歡樂的感覺，而且榴槤的外皮太多刺了。 在台灣，水果禮盒非常普遍，幾乎什麼場合都可以送。大部分賣水果的攤販都能夠幫你準備水果禮盒，只需告訴他們你想送的水果以及要花多少錢，其餘的他們都會幫你打點好。 一個托盤模子會置於盒子的底部，它可以避免水果動來動去，造成還沒有送達目的地之前就受損(沒有人想要的一盒撞傷或壓壞的水果)。接著蓋子會用膠帶固定，東西才不會掉出來。現在你可以準備去送禮了。 下次你必須送禮時，何不選盒水果呢？水果是健康的食物，而且相較之下算是便宜的。也許它不是每個人心目中最理想的禮物，但大部分人都會很喜歡的，因為大家都愛吃水果。
answers: A box of fruit is a common gift in Taiwan.
Whatever is in season is probably the best.
It prevents the fruit from moving around.

4.title: Puffed Rice
paras: Somewhere in Yuanlin on the side of the street, you may see this lady making puffed rice squares. To make such squares, rice, wheat or other grains are put into a pressure cooker (the old black thing with a wheel on the left side). The contents are then heated, causing the air pressure inside the cooker to increase dramatically. When the air pressure reaches a certain point, the contents pop—much the same way as popcorn does. Once the grain is popped, it is mixed with other ingredients to make it sweet and sticky. Then it is pressed into a wooden mold and cut into squares.

The lady, shown here, is cutting the squares with a sharp knife. She uses a flat piece of wood as a guide to make her cuts nice and straight. To her left you can see the finished squares, bagged and ready for sale. They look scrumptious!
questions: Where might you see this lady making puffed rice squares?
What kind of machine does she use to make puffed grains?
What makes/causes the rice puff up?
4.translation: 在員林某處的街道旁，你也許會看到這位女士在製作爆米香塊。若要製作這種爆米香，需將米、小麥或其他穀物放入壓力鍋(那個黑色、老舊而且左邊有方向盤的東西)。接下來把鍋子裡面的東西加熱，這會導致鍋子裡面的氣壓急劇上升。當壓力到達某個點後，裡面的東西就會發出像爆米花一樣砰的一聲。一旦裡頭的穀物爆開後，便可加入其他配料，使它變得又甜又黏。接著放到一個木製的模型壓平，然後再切成方塊。 此處可看到這位女士正用一把銳利的刀子切著爆米花。她用一根平整的木頭來引導她切得很直。你可以在她的左邊看到切好的方塊，它們已經裝袋準備好要賣了。它們看起來非常好吃的樣子。
answers: You might see her somewhere in Yuanlin on the side of the street.
She uses a pressure cooker to make puffed grains.
Heat and air pressure makes the rice puff up.

4.title: Whack-A-Mole
paras: Quentin is a very angry 14 year old boy. He is constantly in the principal’s office at school for starting fights in the hallway and for disobeying his teacher. He has so much trouble controlling his temper in fact, that his parents have even taken him to a psychologist. But to no avail.
There is one thing, however, that helps Quentin to release his anger in a less violent way. It is a game called Whack-a-Mole.

Whack-a-Mole is an arcade type game. The object is to hit the mole as it comes out of its hole. The more moles you hit, or “whack”, the more points you get and the longer you can play, but it isn’t as easy as you think.

The moles pop up and down very quickly. There are six different holes, too and you never know which hole a mole is going to come out of. Plus, the longer the game goes on, the faster the moles go up and down. You definitely need good hand-eye coordination to play.

When Quentin plays the game, he releases a lot of pent-up energy. After he is finished, he is always very calm. Perhaps his school should consider putting a Whack-a-Mole machine in his classroom. If nothing else, it would prevent him from playing Whack-a-Classmate or, worse yet, Whack-a-Teacher.
questions: Why is Quentin constantly in the principal’s office?
What helps him to release his anger?
What happens the longer the game goes on?
4.translation: 昆丁十四歲，是個愛生氣的男孩。他經常因為在走廊找人打架，或是不服老師的管教，而被叫到校長室。事實上，他很難控制自己的脾氣，以致父母甚至曾經帶他去看心理醫師，但是卻沒有作用。 然而，有個方法能幫他用比較溫和的方式來釋放他的怒氣，那是一種叫做打地鼠的遊戲。 打地鼠是電動遊樂場那一類型的遊戲，目標是打擊從洞裡冒出來的地鼠。你打(或是「重擊」)的地鼠愈多，得的分數就愈多，也能夠玩得比較久一點。但這個遊戲並沒有如你想像中的容易。 地鼠出現和消失的速度很快，而且它有六個不同的洞，你從不知道地鼠會從哪一個洞冒出來。此外，遊戲進行愈久，地鼠出現與消失的速度就愈快。玩這種遊戲，你的手眼協調性絕對要很好。 當昆丁玩打地鼠時，他可以釋放很多壓抑在心底的能量。當遊戲結束時，他總是感到很平靜。也許他的學校應該考慮在他的教室放一台打地鼠的遊戲機，若不是為了其他的因素，至少可以避免昆丁變成「打同學」，或者更慘的是─「打老師」。
answers: He is constantly in the principal’s office for starting fights in the hallway and disobeying his teacher.
A game called Whack-a-Mole helps him to release his anger.
The longer the game goes on, the faster the moles go up and down.

4.title: Using the Printer
paras: Jenny needs to print off a few pictures for a social studies project. She is almost ready to start printing when she notices that the printer is out of paper. Luckily, she had just bought a package of paper the day before at an office supply store.

She digs the package of paper out from under the clutter in her backpack and removes the wrapping. Not wanting the paper to get wrinkled or torn, she slowly removes a half a dozen sheets from the pack.

Next Jenny lifts the flap on the front of the printer and carefully feeds the paper into the tray until it won’t go any further. Then she closes the lid again and returns to her seat at the workstation.

The pictures she wants to print are already on the desktop and ready to go. She hits the print button in the picture viewer window and the printer menu pops up. Here she selects the number of copies she wants and clicks on the okay button.

The printer begins to hum and the ink cartridges dance back and forth inside the printer as it prepares to print. A single sheet of paper begins to disappear inside the machine. Once again, the ink cartridges are moving, this time printing a picture.

In no time at all, the paper can be seen re-entering the outside world, but now it is full of color. Picture after picture are printed off and drop onto the table. As the last of the pictures is finished, the printer returns to standby mode. Jenny shuffles through the papers to make sure they are all there and then dashes off to class.
questions: What does Jenny need the pictures for?
What did she buy at the office supply store?
What does the printer do when it is finished?
4.translation: 珍妮的社會科報告需要印一些照片。當她幾乎準備好要開始列印時，她注意到印表機上的紙張已用完了。幸運的是，她前一天剛在辦公室用品店買了一包紙。 她從背包裡雜亂的東西下取出一包紙並且拆開包裝。為了不讓紙張皺摺或是破損，她慢慢地從這包紙當中取出六張紙。 接著她掀開印表機前面的蓋子，然後小心的將紙張放入托盤內，並推到最底部。接著她再次闔上蓋子，然後回到她電腦的座位上。 她想要列印的照片已經放在電腦桌面上，而且準備好了。她在照片觀看的視窗中按下列印鈕，列印的選單就出現了。在選單中，她選擇了想要列印的份數，然後按下OK 的按鈕。 當準備要列印時，印表機開始發出嗡翁的聲音，然後墨水夾在印表機裡來回移動。一張紙開始消失在印表機內，接著墨水夾再次移動，這一次印出一張照片。 很快地這張紙又重新出現在外面的世界，不過現在是色彩豐富的。照片一張接一張列印出來，而且掉在桌上。當最後一張照片完成時，印表機又回到待機模式。珍妮翻閱這些紙以確定全部的照片都印出來了，然後才衝去上課。
answers: She needs them for social studies.
She bought a package of paper.
It goes into standby mode.

5.title: Classroom Cleanup
paras: The elementary students in this class are cleaning up their room. Two boys in the back left are cleaning the blinds with a duster that has a long handle. A girl squatting on the shelf is cleaning the windows. One of the girls is bent over using a dustpan to pick up the excess liquid. The rest of the students and their teacher are looking after the floor.

Even with daily sweeping and mopping, the floor still becomes quite dirty over time. Once a semester, the students have to give the floor a heavy-duty scrubbing. The work is all done by hand. They use mops and scrub brushes in addition to water mixed with cleaning solution. They must scrub very hard to remove the build-up of dirt left behind from previous spills and messes.

Everyone is hard at work and yet the teacher seems to be standing still. She is covering her nose because of the fumes of the cleaning solution. The students wear masks because of this, too and because there is dust floating around.

It is important that no one goofs around while cleaning the wet slippery floor or someone might fall. Still, they need to work fast, so that they can get back to their studies. But as long as everyone pitches in and does their part, it shouldn’t take too long.
questions: What do the boys use to clean the blinds?
Why does the duster have a long handle?
Why do the students wear dust masks?
5.translation: 這個教室的小學生正在打掃他們的教室。左後方的兩位男生正拿著長柄刷清理百葉窗，一個蹲在書櫃上的女生正在擦窗戶，其中一個女生彎腰用畚斗裝多餘的水，其餘的學生和老師則是在清理地板。 即使每天都掃地及拖地，地板在過了一段時間後，仍然會變得很髒。每學期，學生總是要好好地刷一次地板。這個工作都是要用手完成的。除了使用和水混合的清潔劑之外，他們還用拖把及硬毛刷。他們必須用力地刷洗，才能去除之前飲料溢出和髒亂不斷累積所留下的污垢。 每個人都很認真地工作，但老師似乎站著不動。她掩著鼻子，因為清潔劑有著難聞的煙味。學生們也因此戴上了口罩，而另一個原因則是灰塵到處飛揚。 在清掃又溼又滑的地板時，確保沒有人在玩耍嬉戲是很重要的，否則可能有人會跌倒。然而，他們的動作必須要快，以便能夠恢復上課。但只要每個人同心協力並盡到自己的本份，就應該不會花太多的時間。
answers: They use a duster.
It has a long handle, so that the boys can reach higher than normal.
They wear dust masks because of the fumes and because there is dust floating around.

5.title: Food Courts
paras: Many large shopping centers and malls have areas where you can eat. These places are called food courts. They are designed to keep people in shopping centers longer—a hungry customer isn’t going to stick around if there is nothing to eat.

Food courts usually have many independent vendors along the outside and large eating areas in the middle with tables and chairs. First you choose what you want to eat and then you find a place to sit. It is a good idea if you are eating out with friends because you can all eat different restaurants while sitting at the same table.

Types of foods vary greatly and cater to many tastes. Chinese, Italian, Japanese and American foods are the most common and can be found in food courts throughout the world. There are so many choices that even the fussiest eater should be able to find something for lunch.

Food courts may not be a fine dining experience, but they are convenient. They are also usually cheap and relatively clean. More importantly, though, they will fill you up and get you back shopping in no time.
questions: 1. What are the areas in shopping centers where you eat called?
Where is the eating area?
Where are the vendors located?
5.translation: 很多大型的購物中心都有一個可以讓你吃東西的地方，這個地方稱為美食街。美食街的設計是要讓人在購物中心停留久一點：如果沒有東西吃，肚子餓的顧客是不會留下來的。 通常沿著美食街外緣有許多單獨經營的攤販，而美食街的中間有擺放桌椅的大型飲食區。首先，你先選擇你想要吃的東西，然後找個位置坐下來。若你和朋友們一起出去吃的話，美食街是個很好的選擇，因為你可以坐在同一張桌子，吃到不同餐廳的食物。 食物的種類非常多樣化，而且迎合很多種口味。中國、義大利、日本，以及美國的食物是最普遍的，全世界的美食街都可以找到這些食物。這裡的選擇如此之多，就連最挑剔的食客也應該可以找到午餐吃。 雖然在美食街用餐可能不是一個很棒的體驗，但是卻非常的方便。美食街的食物通常很便宜，而且相較之下還算乾淨。然而，更重要的是，他們能使你吃飽，然後很快地再回去購物。
answers: They are called food courts.
The eating area is in the middle of the food court.
They are usually located along the outside of the food court.

5.title: Awards Presentation
paras: My name is Luka (the cute girl with the biggest trophy) and I love memorizing English vocabulary. I don't know why, but it is very fun. My friends think it is weird and like to tease me about it. Sometimes it bothers me, but I know that my strange fascination will help me in the future.

A while back my school decided to hold a vocabulary contest. I immediately signed up. There was no way I could miss an opportunity to prove to my friends that my obsession was worthwhile. Luckily for me, I walked away with the top spot in the competition.
The awards presentation took place two weeks after the competition because the judges needed time to mark the written test. The ceremony took place at my junior high school in one of the classrooms.

The top ten places in the competition each received an award certificate and a red envelope. In addition, the top three got trophies. My trophy was the biggest because I got first place. It was plastic with a tacky angel on the top, but at least I got something to prove to my friends that I won. Now maybe they will stop teasing me all the time.
questions: Why do Luka’s friends tease her?
How did she do in the competition?
Did everyone get a certificate and a red envelope?
answers: They tease her because she likes to memorize English vocabulary.
She won it.
No, only the top 10 places got them.

5.title: Shopping
paras: Carrie loves nothing better than to spend the day shopping. This is one of her favorite clothing stores, called Thrifty Girl. As the name implies, it is for those who are a little cheap. Carrie comes here almost every chance she gets and will spend hours searching the racks for bargains.
Picture 1 Look! Carrie has found a white, hooded sweater. It is so pretty. Holding it up to admire it, she hopes that it fits because there are no more in any size.

Picture 2 Taking the sweater by the hanger, Carrie walks to the change room to try it on. She knocks on the door first to make sure that the small booth is not occupied. No one responds, so she opens the door.

Picture 3 The mirror attached to the front of the door is a good place to see how she looks in the sweater. Turning from side to side as if she were modeling, she scrutinizes every inch of the sweater. Carrie is quite pleased with the look and feel of the new sweater. Back into the change room she goes to change back into her old clothes.
Now that she has a new article of clothing, her shopping addiction is satisfied—for the time being at least. She takes the hoody to the cashier to pay and then happily exits the store.
questions: How often does Carrie go to Thrifty Girl?
How long does she usually spend there?
Why does she turn from side to side in front of the mirror?
5.translation: 凱莉喜愛花時間購物勝過做任何其他的事情。這是她最愛的其中一間服飾店，店名叫做「節儉的女孩」。就如同這個名字的意思一樣，這是為了那些有一點小氣的女孩所開的。凱莉只要有機會就會來這裡，而且會花上好幾個小時找尋架上的便宜貨。 圖片1 你看！凱莉已經找到一件白色有風帽的毛衣，這件毛衣真是漂亮。她把它拿高好好的欣賞一番。她希望這件毛衣可以合身，因為沒有其他的尺寸了。 圖片2 凱莉抓住衣架，帶著毛衣走進更衣室試穿。她先敲門以確定這個小房間沒有人使用。因為沒有人回應，所以她就打開門。 圖片3 裝在門前的鏡子是個觀看穿上毛衣的好地方。彷彿模特兒在作服裝展示，她側身轉來轉去，仔細地查看毛衣的每個地方。凱莉非常滿意這件新毛衣的外觀和質感。她回去更衣室換回去她的舊衣服。 因為有一件新的衣服，她的購物癮得以滿足了─至少目前是這個樣子。她帶著這件有風帽的毛衣去付款，然後很開心地離開這家服飾店。
answers: She goes there every chance she gets.
She usually spends hours there searching the racks for bargains.
She turns from side to side to see how it looks.

5.title: Video Games
paras: I have always wanted to play tennis. Ever since I can remember, the sport has fascinated me. But like everything in my life, I just look from the outside and never get involved.
“Why don’t you just try it out?” my friends say to me. My response is always the same. “I am too shy.”

My shy nature seems to keep me from trying a lot of new things. It isn’t that I don’t want to do new things, it’s just that I am nervous and I don’t want people to see me. I’m afraid they will laugh at me or tease me.

Thankfully someone invented interactive video games. Now that I have a Wii, I can try all kinds of new sports without ever having to leave the house. I can play tennis, golf, table tennis and I can even dance. This great technology really makes you feel like you are a part of the action. You even get some exercise while you play. The best part is I can do all of this in the comfort of my own home.

My father says that I shouldn’t play it so much. He thinks that I need to get outside more or else my shyness will get worse. He also says that I need to spend more time on my homework and less time playing games.

My grades have been slipping ever since I got the Wii, but this is only because I am not studying 24 hours a day now. He doesn’t understand that I need to relax and have fun sometimes. If he had his way, I would have a pencil in one hand and a book in the other all day long. He has even threatened to take my Wii away if my grades don’t improve. He just doesn’t realize the value of video games. One day I will be a virtual tennis star. And what good will all that time wasted studying be to me then? My father just doesn’t understand reality.
questions: How long has tennis fascinated her?
Why doesn’t she like to try new things?
Why does she like her Wii?
5.translation: 我一直都很想要打網球；打從我有記憶以來，網球總是讓我著迷。但就像我生命中的每件事一樣，我都只是旁觀者，從未親身參與。 「為什麼你不試試看呢？」我的朋友對我說。而我的回答總是一樣的：「我太害羞了。」 我害羞的本性似乎讓我無法嘗試很多的新事物。並不是我不想嘗試新事物，只是我會很緊張，而且我不想有人看著我，我很怕他們會取笑我或嘲諷我。 感恩的是，有人發明了互動式的電視遊樂器。既然有了Wii，我就可以不必出門而嘗試各式各樣的新運動。我可以打網球、高爾夫球、桌球，我甚至還可以跳舞呢！這項偉大的發明真的讓你感到身歷其境，在玩的過程中甚至還能運動呢！最棒的是，我可以在自己的家裡輕鬆自在地做這件事。 我爸爸說我不應該那麼常玩，他認為我需要多出去走走，不然我害羞的個性會愈來愈嚴重。他也說我需要花更多的時間寫作業，並且少玩電視遊樂器。 自從我有了Wii之後，我的成績就一直退步。不過，這只是因為我不再是一天24小時都在讀書罷了。我爸並不明白，有時候我也需要休息和娛樂。如果照他的意思，我一整天都會是一手拿筆，而另一手拿書的。他甚至威脅，如果我的成績沒有進步的話，就要沒收我的Wii。他就是不了解電視遊樂器的價值。總有一天，我會成為一位虛擬的網球明星。那些浪費在讀書上面的時間對我有什麼好處？我爸爸就是不了解現實。
answers: As long as she can remember, tennis has fascinated her.
She is nervous and doesn’t want people to see her.
She likes it because it makes her feel like a part of the action and she can also get some exercise while doing it.

5.title: Sewing Buttons
paras: Cara’s button has fallen off her jacket. In order to put it back on, she will have to put her sewing skills to the test. Luckily, her mother has a small sewing kit for such occasions.

Perhaps the most difficult part of sewing is threading the needle. It requires good hand-eye coordination to feed the tiny thread through the microscopic eye of the needle. Sometimes it helps to moisten the end of the thread a bit, especially if it is frayed.
“I finally got it!” Cara yells after three hours of trying to thread the needle.

Cara holds the button in place while she pushes the needle through the button hole into the fabric. After pulling the thread all the way to the knot on the end, she turns the needle and pushes it back up through a different hole. She repeats this process many times. When she is sure the button is secure, she ties a knot in the thread and she is finished.

Oops! There seems to be a small problem. Due to a preexisting medical condition, Cara has no feeling in the fingers of her left hand. As a result, she has accidentally sewn her hand to the jacket. Better luck next time, Cara.
questions: What does Cara have to put to the test in order to put the button back on the jacket?
What is perhaps the most difficult part of sewing?
Why is there a knot at the end of the thread?
5.translation: 卡拉的鈕扣從她的夾克掉了下來。為了把它縫回去，她將必須試試她的縫紉技術。幸運地是，她的媽媽有這種情況所需要的小針線包。 也許縫紉最困難的部份就是穿線。要將細小的線穿進微小的針孔裡需要有很好的手眼協調度。有的時候，將線的末端稍微弄溼對穿線有幫助，特別是如果線有磨散的情形。 「我終於辦到了！」卡拉在經歷了三個小時的穿線之後大叫著。 卡拉將鈕扣固定在適當的地方，同時把針推過鈕釦的洞並且穿入布料。她把線一直拉到末端的結之後，便把針轉回來，並且從另外一個洞把針推回來。這個過程重複很多次。當她確定鈕扣已經縫牢了之後，她把線打了一個結，然後就大功告成了。 糟糕！似乎有一個小問題。由於先前的健康情況，卡拉左手的手指是沒有知覺的。因此，她意外地把她的手跟夾克縫在一起了。卡拉，祝妳下次好運囉！
answers: She has to put her sewing skills to the test.
Threading the needle.
There is a knot there to stop the thread from pulling right through the fabric.

5.title: Interior Painting
paras: Painting is not so much fun, but every now and then it has to be done. Walls become dirty or just fade over time and start to look dull and drab. The only thing that will spice them up is a fresh coat of paint. You CAN do it yourself, but why waste your time when you can hire someone to do it for you?

This is the guy to call. He is a professional painter, which means he paints for a living. He can do the job faster and better than most people. It may cost more to hire him than by doing it yourself, but the end result is going to be much better.
Here, he is standing on a stepladder so that he can reach the higher areas. He is holding a paintbrush in his right hand and a can of white paint in his left.

The bottom half of the wall is already finished, but he still has the top half to go. He uses a brush for corners and obstacles, like light switches, fuse panels or anything else that can’t be painted with a roller. For larger areas, it is much quicker for him to use a roller. He also covers up the furniture and puts plastic on part of the floor so that paint doesn’t get splashed or spilled on it.

No matter who paints, you should remember to wear a mask. Paint fumes are quite toxic and are not good to inhale. You can also open the windows in the room to allow fresh air inside. If you paint a bedroom, it is best to let it air out for a couple of days before you sleep in it. You can use it as an excuse to go on a holiday for a couple of days.
questions: Why is it a good idea to hire a professional painter?
What are some of the things he uses to paint with?
Why should you wear a mask?
5.translation: 油漆並不是一件很有趣的事情，但這件事有時還是必須做。時間久了牆壁會變髒或只是褪色，並且開始看起來沒有光澤、了無生氣。能使牆壁變得鮮亮的唯一方法就是為它上一層全新的油漆。你可以自己做這件事情，但是當你可以雇用他人為你做這件事情的時候，為什麼還要浪費自己的時間呢? 若要找人粉刷牆壁打電話給這個人就對了。他是專業的油漆工，也就是說他是以粉刷油漆維生。他可以比大部分的人做得更快更好。雖然雇用他比你自己動手做的開銷還要多，但是最後的結果將會好很多。 此處他正站在工作梯上，如此一來他可以接觸到較高的地方。他的右手正握著油漆刷，而左手提著一桶白色的油漆。牆壁的下半部已經完成了，但是他還有上半部要刷。他使用刷子來漆角落以及電燈開關、保險絲面板這類的障礙物，或是其它無法用滾筒上漆的地方。比較大的區域，他用滾筒刷會更快。他也將傢俱蓋住，並且把塑膠鋪在地板的一些地方，如此油漆才不會濺出或灑到地板上。 不論是誰刷油漆，都得記得戴上口罩。油漆的氣味很毒，吸入了不好。你也可以打開室內的窗戶，讓新鮮的空氣進入。如果你油漆的是臥室，最好在你入住前，先讓空氣流通幾天，你可以利用這個藉口去度假幾天。
answers: It is a good idea because he can do the job faster and better than most people.
He uses a paintbrush and a roller.
You should wear a mask because paint fumes are bad for you.

5.title: Pay and Park
paras: This is a pay-and-park style parking lot. Before you can enter, you must take a ticket from the ticket dispenser. After the ticket is issued, the arm on the gate will lift, allowing you to drive your car inside.
